content.text:
<user_query>
You are helping with BIQc production hardening. Provide a concise enterprise 'zero-drop' architecture plan focused on preventing calibration scan failures after deploy. Include: auth/session architecture, edge function governance, release gates, observability/SLOs, rollback strategy, and security controls. Return: (1) top 10 controls, (2) 30-60-90 day execution roadmap, (3) measurable KPIs.
</user_query>
Grounding the plan in your stack (FastAPI calibration, Supabase edge functions, deploy workflow) with a quick read of key touchpoints.

Here is a concise **zero-drop** plan aimed at **calibration scan reliability after deploy**, aligned with your stack (Azure App Service + ACR, GitHub Actions, Supabase Auth + Edge Functions, FastAPI `/api/calibration/*`).

---

## 1) Top 10 controls (prioritized)

1. **Contract-first calibration path** — Treat “calibration scan” as an end-to-end **SLO-backed capability**: browser → `biqc.ai` → API proxy/nginx → FastAPI → Supabase (DB/RPC) → **named Edge Functions** (e.g. `calibration-*`). Document the **exact URL surface**, required headers (Bearer), and **expected HTTP codes** (you already gate on `/api/calibration/status` returning 401/403 unauthenticated in deploy).

2. **Auth/session single source of truth** — Supabase for identity; backend validates JWT consistently (no drift between “session works on home” vs “calibration fails”). Enforce **redirect URL allowlists**, **token refresh** behavior, and **clock skew** tolerance. Automate **production `site_url` / redirect URLs** as part of release (you already PATCH auth config post-deploy; extend this to **verify**, not only set).

3. **Edge Function governance** — **Versioned deploy** of functions with the same release train as app images (or pinned function “bundle id” in config). **Canary** high-risk functions (`calibration-*`, `business-brain-*`, `intelligence-bridge`, etc.). **JWT verification** on every function; **deny-by-default** CORS; **secrets** only via Supabase secrets; **timeouts and payload limits** explicit.

4. **Release gates (pre + post)** — **Pre:** blocking tests (you have `test_deploy_gate_smoke.py`), plus a **synthetic “calibration happy path”** against **staging** with real auth (service or test user), not only 401 probes. **Post:** extend smoke beyond health/home to **one authenticated calibration read** (or signed internal probe) so deploy failures catch **auth/session/config** regressions.

5. **Configuration immutability per release** — Pin **image SHA** (you do), and pin **frontend build args** (Supabase URL/keys) to the same release record. **Runtime config** (Azure app settings, Supabase project settings) tracked in IaC or a **release manifest** so “silent drift” cannot break scans.

6. **Observability & SLOs** — **RED** metrics per step: `calibration_status`, `calibration_sync`, each Edge Function invocation (latency, 4xx/5xx, cold start). **Correlation IDs** from web → API → function logs. **SLO example:** p95 authenticated calibration status < 2s, error budget burn alerts.

7. **Queue / retry / idempotency** — If scans are async (jobs/Redis/edge queues), enforce **idempotent job keys**, **DLQ**, and **user-visible state** (“queued / running / failed / partial”) so deploys never leave users in a dead state without telemetry.

8. **Rollback strategy** — **Fast:** re-point App Service to **previous known-good image tags** (keep N-1, N-2 in ACR). **Data-safe:** backward-compatible API for 1–2 releases. **Supabase functions:** maintain **previous deployment** or feature flags for critical paths. **Auth:** avoid one-way migrations without dual-read period.

9. **Security controls (enterprise)** — **WAF/rate limits** on public auth and calibration endpoints; **mTLS or signed server-to-server** for backend→Edge where applicable; **least-privilege** Supabase keys (anon vs service role); **audit** admin/config changes; **dependency + container** scanning in CI; **secrets rotation** runbook.

10. **Incident readiness** — Runbook: “calibration scan failure” with **decision tree** (CDN vs App Service vs Supabase vs OpenAI quota). **Blameless postmortems** tied to SLO burn; **feature flags** to disable non-critical scan steps without dropping core calibration.

---

## 2) 30 / 60 / 90 day execution roadmap

**Days 0–30 (stabilize + measure)**  
- Define the **calibration critical user journey** (3–5 API/function steps) and add **dashboards + alerts** for each.  
- Add **staging** parity: same nginx rules, same Supabase project class (or branch), same Edge Function set.  
- Harden CI: make **one** calibration integration test **blocking** on merge to `main` (beyond unauthenticated probes).  
- Document and test **rollback**: rehearse Azure image rollback + Supabase function revert.  
- Lock **post-deploy verification**: extend current smoke (health, `/api/calibration/status` 401/403, integration-status probe) with **authenticated probe** or **internal canary** token.

**Days 31–60 (govern + gate)**  
- Edge Function **inventory**: owners, SLOs, dependencies, max runtime; enforce **code review + deploy checklist**.  
- Implement **canary** for calibration-related functions or traffic split via feature flag.  
- **Correlation ID** propagation end-to-end; **structured logging** with `user_id` hashed / `request_id`.  
- **Error budget** policy: freeze releases when burn exceeds threshold.  
- **WAF/rate limit** rules for `/api/auth/*` and calibration endpoints; tune false positives.

**Days 61–90 (optimize + enterprise)**  
- **Chaos / failure injection** on staging (Supabase latency, function timeout, Redis down).  
- **Automated rollback** on SLO violation within N minutes of deploy (optional with human approval).  
- **Compliance pack**: access reviews, secret rotation calendar, pentest scope including Edge Functions.  
- **Capacity planning**: cold start mitigation (provisioned concurrency if platform supports, or warm-up jobs).

---

## 3) Measurable KPIs

| Area | KPI | Target (example) |
|------|-----|-------------------|
| **Availability** | Success rate of authenticated `GET /api/calibration/status` (or primary read) | ≥ 99.9% monthly |
| **Latency** | p95 / p99 for calibration read + first scan step | p95 < 2s, p99 < 5s (tune to reality) |
| **Edge Functions** | Invocation success rate + timeout rate per function | Success ≥ 99.5%; timeouts < 0.5% |
| **Deploy quality** | Post-deploy smoke pass rate; **MTTR** when smoke fails | 100% required; MTTR < 15 min |
| **Auth** | OAuth callback error rate; token refresh failures | Callback errors < 0.1% of logins |
| **Drift** | Incidents caused by misconfigured `site_url`/redirect/env mismatch | 0 per quarter after automation |
| **Queue (if async)** | Jobs stuck > T minutes; DLQ depth | 0 sustained backlog; DLQ cleared < 24h |
| **Security** | Mean time to patch critical CVEs in images; failed auth spike detection | Critical < 7 days; alert on 3σ spike |
| **Rollback** | Time to complete known-good rollback drill | < 30 minutes |
| **User impact** | % sessions completing calibration scan without retry | Track weekly; trend ↑ |

---

**Note:** Your pipeline already encodes part of this philosophy (image pin verification, health checks, production `/api/calibration/status` auth gate, Supabase `site_url` lock). The largest **zero-drop** gap is usually **authenticated calibration path + Edge Function SLOs** in the same release train as the containers—closing that loop turns “deploy green” into “scans actually work for signed-in users.”
